name: Barbados
avoid: June–November is wetter and overlaps the Atlantic storm season, so cave tours, catamaran trips, and east-coast viewpoints are more likely to be affected by passing systems.
Crop Over events from late July into early August raise south-coast rates and make Oistins, Bridgetown, and beach buses noticeably busier.
Bathsheba's Atlantic side is gorgeous but often rough for swimming, so treat it more as a surf-and-scenery stop unless conditions are clearly calm and lifeguards are present.
stopoverNote: For trips under 6 days, most travelers stay on the south or west coast and visit Bathsheba and Speightstown as day trips instead of changing hotels repeatedly.

cities.name: Bridgetown
cities.notes: Historic Bridgetown is the practical arrival base for UNESCO core sights, Carlisle Bay beaches, rum heritage, and easy island-wide bus connections.

cities.name: Bathsheba
cities.notes: Barbados' dramatic east-coast stop for Soup Bowl surf, Bathsheba rock formations, breezy viewpoints, and a slower village feel.

cities.name: Speightstown
cities.notes: A calmer west-coast heritage town with waterfront walks, local rum history nearby, and easier beach time than the Atlantic side.

cities.name: Oistins
cities.notes: The south-coast favorite for Miami Beach, the famous fish fry, guesthouse-heavy stays, and quick bus access to the airport and Bridgetown.

itinerary.sim: Digicel Barbados or FLOW prepaid data packs are the easiest local options; budget roughly ₹1,500–₹2,500 for a short-stay data plan and keep offline maps handy for east-coast drives.

connections.method: Transport Board bus or ZR minibus across the island via ABC Highway / east-coast roads (about 45–60m)
Rental car is fastest via inland roads (about 60–75m); by bus you usually connect back through Bridgetown
connections.cost: ₹147–₹294 pp by bus; ₹5,000–₹7,500/day for a rental car

connections.method: Transport Board bus, ZR minibus, or rental car down the west and south coasts (about 60–75m)

extras: Transport Board buses use a flat BBD$3.50 fare per ride, which works out to about ₹147; carry exact cash for blue government buses and ZR minibuses.
ZR minibuses are the quickest cheap option on many main routes, but they can be fast and crowded; choose blue Transport Board buses if you want more space and easier luggage handling.

Rental cars make Bathsheba, St. Nicholas Abbey, and scattered west-coast beaches much easier; Barbados drives on the left and rental agencies usually issue the local driving permit for about BBD$10 (roughly ₹420).
December through April is Barbados' most bookable stretch, so smaller guesthouses in Bathsheba and Oistins often need advance reservations several weeks out.
Use caution in Bathsheba and other east-coast surf zones: currents and shore break are stronger than on the calmer Carlisle Bay and Miami Beach sides.
